title: Crypto Portfolio App with Internet Identity, Manual Holdings, Live Prices, and Performance

requirements.text: Implement user authentication using Internet Identity and require users to be signed in before they can view or modify any portfolio data.

quotes: User must autenticate with Internet identity

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users see a sign-in prompt and cannot access portfolio screens.
Authenticated users remain signed in across refreshes until they sign out.
A visible sign-out control is available when signed in.

requirements.text: Allow authenticated users to create, view, edit, and delete their crypto holdings, where holdings are user-entered (no exchange sync). Each holding must include at minimum: asset identifier (symbol and/or coin id), quantity, and an optional cost basis (total cost or average buy price) to enable gains/loss calculations.

quotes: 1 only users entered Holding with live coin price.

acceptanceCriteria: Users can add a new holding and it persists to the backend under their identity.
Users can edit quantity and cost basis fields and see updates reflected after reload.
Users can delete a holding and it no longer appears after reload.
Holdings are isolated per authenticated user (one user cannot see another user’s holdings).

requirements.text: Fetch live coin prices from an external public price API and use them to compute each holding’s current value and the total portfolio value.

quotes: 1 only users entered Holding with live coin price.

acceptanceCriteria: Each holding shows a live price and current value (quantity × live price).
The portfolio shows a total current value across all holdings.
If the price API is unavailable, the UI shows a clear English error state and retains the last successfully fetched prices in the UI for the current session (if available).

requirements.text: Display portfolio performance for authenticated users including per-asset and total gains/losses (both absolute and percentage) using current values and user-entered cost basis.

quotes: 2. With portfolio performance like gains,losses wit current values

acceptanceCriteria: For holdings with cost basis provided, the UI shows gain/loss amount and gain/loss percentage.
The UI shows total gain/loss amount and total gain/loss percentage across all holdings with cost basis.
Holdings without cost basis are clearly indicated and excluded from gain/loss calculations (but still included in current total value).

requirements.text: Add at least one simple performance visualization (e.g., allocation by asset and/or gains vs. losses summary) suitable for the available data (manual holdings + live prices + cost basis).

quotes: With portfolio performance like gains,losses wit current values

acceptanceCriteria: At least one chart/visual summary renders correctly on desktop and mobile sizes.
The visualization updates when holdings are added/edited/deleted or when prices refresh.

requirements.text: Create and apply a cohesive visual theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, component styling) and use it consistently across authentication, holdings, and performance screens. Do not use a blue/purple primary palette.
acceptanceCriteria: All key screens share a consistent styling system (buttons, cards, tables/forms, headings).
The primary palette is not blue/purple.
UI is responsive and readable on mobile and desktop.

requirements.text: Add generated static image assets required by the UI (logo and a subtle background/hero graphic) and render them from the frontend as static files (do not serve images from the backend).
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist under frontend/public/assets/generated and are referenced by the UI.
Images load correctly in the running app without backend endpoints.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (no additional backend services).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui or other immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose them instead.
Use English for all user-facing UI text.
nonGoals: Exchange account linking or automatic import of holdings from centralized exchanges.
On-chain wallet integrations or Web3 wallet connections.
Real-time streaming prices via websockets; periodic polling is sufficient.

required: Minimal geometric coin/portfolio logo mark, flat vector style (portfolio-logo.dim_512x512.png)
Subtle abstract background/hero graphic with warm-neutral palette, soft gradients, no text (portfolio-hero-bg.dim_1600x900.png)
